stemHtml: Which one of the following statements correctly describes how a cold-air drop is formed?

options.a: In summer, the air behind a cold front can slow down as the primary system becomes weaker. In this case surface heating results in a fall in pressure, which creates a small area of lower pressure known as a cold-air drop.
options.b: Air behind a slow-moving warm front passes over a cold surface. In this case the cooling of the air above can result in a localised pool of cold air forming, and a corresponding fall in pressure. This is known as a cold-air drop.
options.c: A large mass of polar air moves to a lower latitude and becomes surrounded by warmer air. This creates a low-pressure centre above the surface, known as cold-air drop.
options.d: A cold-air drop is formed when a large low-pressure centre is created above the surface descent of very cold air within a warm anticyclone.
